description: Migrate the Confluence plugin to use SDK utilities: replace manual token persistence with getAuthCache/setAuthCache, replace manual waitForAuth with waitUntil, and replace local getMetaContent helper with SDK getMetaContent.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/confluence && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Migrate Confluence plugin to SDK utilities
userStories.description: Replace Confluence's manual token persistence, waitForAuth, and local getMetaContent helper with SDK equivalents.
acceptanceCriteria: Local getMetaContent function removed — replaced by getMetaContent from SDK
getPersistedAuth/setPersistedAuth replaced with getAuthCache/setAuthCache using namespace 'confluence'
waitForAuth uses waitUntil wrapped to return boolean
No setInterval or clearInterval calls remain in auth code
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/confluence/src/confluence-api.ts

Context:
1. Local getMetaContent at lines 11-12:
```typescript
const getMetaContent = (name: string): string | null =>
  document.querySelector(`meta[name="${name}"]`)?.getAttribute('content') ?? null;
```
Delete this and import getMetaContent from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk'.

2. Token persistence at lines 14-33 — replace with getAuthCache/setAuthCache using namespace 'confluence'.

3. waitForAuth at lines 56-73 — standard polling pattern.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change isAuthenticated() logic beyond using SDK getMetaContent
- Do NOT change apiV1() or apiV2() or any tool files
- getMetaContent is also used in getAccountId() export — ensure it still works after switching to SDK import
